rationale: The cited paper is used as a central prior finding in the citing paper's contagion framework, not just as a passing background reference. The authors explicitly claim they extend contagion research beyond contact with disgusting products (citing Morales and Fitzsimons 2007) to superficial packaging damage, and they draw on its contact-visualization logic when motivating their buffer manipulation. They also revisit this comparison in the general discussion to position contributions and boundary conditions against prior contamination results. This indicates a clear, meaningful conceptual role.
relation_description: The citing paper builds on Morales and Fitzsimons (2007) by extending product contagion to a new cue: superficial packaging damage. It keeps the core contamination account but adds new process and boundary evidence (automatic processing, health/safety inferences, branding mitigation, and physical buffering).
